app.title: Hermes Orchestration War Room
app.description: Visual orchestration layer for the Hermes multi-agent delegation system.

detail.title: Operative dossier
detail.taskFeedNotStarted: Worker hasn't started yet — no log file on disk.
detail.taskFeedNoSession: Worker started but session ID not yet stamped in the log.
detail.taskFeedLogHint: Showing the last lines of the raw worker log — useful when state.db hasn't caught up yet.
detail.taskKillConfirmBody: Terminates the worker process (SIGTERM) and archives the task so it disappears from the active board. Recoverable via `hermes kanban` if you need to inspect it later.
detail.approveBody: The worker hit a Hermes permission classifier and was auto-denied. Approve the dangerous-pattern label below to add it to this profile's `command_allowlist` and retry the task.
detail.approveLabelHint: Exact label as Hermes' classifier emits it. Edit if the suggestion looks wrong before approving.
detail.approveNoLabel: Could not extract a label — type it manually.

mission.title: Mission
mission.dispatcherWarning: No dispatcher detected. Run `hermes gateway start` so workers pick up tasks.

input.supervisor: Comment or request adjustments while the team works…

triage.label: /triage
triage.description: Let Hermes decompose the task automatically

triageDraft.title: Brief ready for triage
triageDraft.subtitle: Review or edit the brief before launching it to the kanban.
triageDraft.launchedBody: No subtasks | 1 subtask created | {count} subtasks created
triageDraft.checkPanel: Brief prepared — review and launch it from the panel below.

directTriage.launched: Triage decomposed into subtasks — the team is on it.

supervisor.note: Triage `{id}` in flight · the orchestrator is supervising the team.

board.boardEmpty: Nothing on the board yet — assign a mission to get started.

action.specifySuccess: Brief rewritten and promoted to todo

detail.title: Operative dossier
detail.noMission: No active mission for this operative.

hire.title: Operative intake
hire.subtitle: Pick a preset, edit the dossier, deploy.
hire.presetHint: Each preset prefills identity, rules, tools and skills. You can tweak everything before hiring.
hire.nameHint: Lowercase letters and numbers only — used as the kanban assignee value.
hire.cloneHint: Optionally seed config.yaml + .env from an existing profile. Identity below overrides their SOUL.md.
hire.noteAfterCreate: MCP servers and provider auth can be configured after the profile is created.

orchestrator.title: Orchestrator
orchestrator.tagline: You need dispatch, routing, drift detection, and escalation.

builder.title: Builder
builder.tagline: You need product code shipped with tests and build proof.

reviewer.title: Reviewer
reviewer.tagline: You need byte-verified review and merge readiness.

triage.title: Triage
triage.tagline: You need issues and PRs scored, reproduced, patched, prepared.

lab.title: Lab
lab.tagline: You need isolated experiments or local-model benchmarking.

fetcher.title: Data Fetcher
fetcher.tagline: You need information pulled from the open web — search, scrape, structured extraction. No analysis.

sage.title: Sage
sage.tagline: You need research, synthesis, scripts, or launch copy.

scribe.title: Scribe
scribe.tagline: You need docs, specs, handoffs, skills hygiene, memory curation.

foundation.title: Foundation
foundation.tagline: You need runtime, repair, infra, health, or lifecycle work.

qa.title: QA
qa.tagline: You need regression, smoke, expected-vs-actual verification.

mirror.title: Mirror Integrations
mirror.tagline: You need upstream sync, integrations, or asset packs.

custom.title: Custom
custom.tagline: You're creating a lane that does not fit an existing preset.

soul.title: Identity
soul.hint: Free-form Markdown saved to SOUL.md inside the profile. Tells the agent who they are and what they do.
soul.placeholder:
# Who You Are

_You're not a chatbot. You're becoming someone._

Who Am I?

- **Name:**
- **Role:**
- **Vibe:**

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.hint: Remote tool sources connected to this profile. Failing servers slow every cold start (3 retries, ~7s wasted) — remove the ones this profile doesn't use.
mcp.empty: No MCP servers configured for this profile.
mcp.confirmRemove: Remove MCP server "{name}" from this profile? Re-adding requires the Hermes CLI.
mcp.headersTooltip: Number of HTTP headers configured (likely auth tokens). Their values are not shown.

retrain.title: Retrain {name}
retrain.subtitle: Identity & rules on the left, tools & knowledge on the right. Save when done.

rename.label: Profile slug
rename.help: Lowercase alphanumeric. Renames the Hermes profile and re-points workers to the new id.
rename.invalid: Slug must be lowercase letters and numbers only.

fire.confirmBody: This permanently removes the Hermes profile, its config, SOUL, AGENTS rules, skills and kanban data. This cannot be undone.

profileConfig.modelHint: The `model.default` value Hermes resolves through its model registry (e.g. `qwen3.6`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `gpt-4-turbo`).
profileConfig.modelSearch: Search or type a model id…
profileConfig.modelEmpty: Catalog empty — type any model id.
profileConfig.inheritGlobalHint: Use the provider and model from `~/.hermes/config.yaml`. Toggle off to override per-profile.
profileConfig.providerHint: Inference provider (`anthropic`, `openai`, `custom`, …). Leave empty to inherit from the global Hermes config.
profileConfig.allowlistHint: Commands matching these dangerous-pattern descriptions skip the user-approval prompt. For an orchestrator, leave this EMPTY so things like `python3 -c` get auto-denied.
profileConfig.allowlistEmpty: No pre-approvals — every dangerous command will go through the permission callback (which the war-room denies by default).

agents.title: Behavior rules (AGENTS.md)
agents.hintGlobal: This profile has no local AGENTS.md — you're seeing the global ~/.hermes/AGENTS.md. Saving creates a profile-specific copy that overrides it.
agents.hintProfile: Profile-specific AGENTS.md. Loaded into the agent's system prompt at every turn.
agents.hintEmpty: No rules defined globally or for this profile. Add behavior conventions, dos & don'ts, etc.
agents.placeholder:
# Behavior rules

- Never commit without explicit confirmation.
- Prefer reading before writing.
- Use plain language for non-technical reports.

description.label: Operative description
description.hint: Short summary of what this operative is used for. Hermes' decomposer reads it to route new tasks to the best-fit profile.
description.placeholder: e.g. "legal researcher: tracks regulatory changes in BOE and summarises into actionable notes"…
description.empty: No description — the decomposer will route by name only.
description.autoTitle: Generate description automatically via the auxiliary LLM

whatsNew.title: New triage + auto-decompose flow
whatsNew.lead: Hermes just changed how the kanban gets orchestrated. The War Room now delegates subtask routing to Hermes itself instead of asking the orchestrator to dispatch them one-by-one.

slash.title: /triage shortcut
slash.body: Type "/" in the mission chat and a command menu appears. /triage drops the prompt straight into Hermes' triage column and decomposes the mission, skipping the conversation entirely.

refine.title: Refine, then launch when ready
refine.body: Without the shortcut, you chat with the orchestrator to refine the brief. When it's solid, a "Brief ready for triage" panel appears with editable title and description; clicking "Launch" makes the War Room fire create+decompose for you.

descriptions.title: Operative descriptions drive routing
descriptions.body: From each operative's dossier you can edit (or auto-generate) its description. That's the signal Hermes' decomposer reads to route each subtask to the best-fit profile.

kanban.title: New Triage column on the board
kanban.body: Rough ideas parked in the Triage column expose "Decompose" and "Specify" actions per card, so you can promote them into the active flow without leaving the War Room.
